questionHtml:
What is secreted by the pancreas for digestion in the small intestine?

A.  Lipase to break bond between phosphate and fatty acid

B.  Insulin to digest glycogen to glucose

C.  Amylopectin to digest starch to maltose

D.  Endopeptidase to digest proteins to peptides

meta.title: What is secreted by the pancreas for digestion in the small intestine? A. Lipase to break bond between phosphate and fatty acid B. Insulin to digest glycogen to glucose C. Amylopec
breadcrumbLabels: D3.3.3. Regulation of blood glucose as an example of the role of hormones in homeostasis
